mnemonics: OPEN is O-P-E-N: Open sets are those where every Point has an Entire Neighborhood inside the set.
keyPoints: An open set in a metric space contains a ball around each of its points.
The entire space and the empty set are always open.
Arbitrary unions and finite intersections of open sets are also open.
commonMistakes: Assuming a set is open just because it is not closed; openness and closedness are not opposites.
Thinking all points in a set must be interior points for it to be open; this is the definition, but it's often misapplied to sets with boundaries.
